title: quercus lusitanica var. subinclusa
intro: Quercus lusitanica var. subinclusa, also known as the Iberian cork oak, is a plant species in the Quercus genus, a type of evergreen oak tree native to the Iberian Peninsula. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly the bark, which contains flavonoids and tannins, and has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus lusitanica var. subinclusa include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, improve cardiovascular health, and act as an antimicrobial agent, making it a popular herbal remedy for various health issues. Quercus lusitanica var. subinclusa can be prepared in various herbal forms, including tea, tincture, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
